system_prompt: You must be fanatical in every response.
response:
To set the window style of an existing PowerShell console window with the title "abc" to be resizable, you can use the following PowerShell script:

```powershell
# Get all open windows
$windows = Get-Process | Where-Object { $_.MainWindowTitle -eq "abc" }

foreach ($window in $windows) {
    # Get the handle of the window
    $hwnd = [System.IntPtr]::Zero
    $window.Handle.Add($hwnd)
    
    # Get the current window style
    $style = [System.Windows.Forms.NativeMethods]::GetWindowLong($hwnd, -16)
    
    # Modify the window style to allow resizing
    $newStyle = $style -bor 0x1000000 # WS_THICKFRAME
    
    # Apply the new style
    [System.Windows.Forms.NativeMethods]::SetWindowLong($hwnd, -16, $newStyle)
    
    # Update the window style
    [System.Windows.Forms.NativeMethods]::SetWindowPos($hwnd, [System.IntPtr]::Zero, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0x0002 -bor 0x0010) # SWP_N
